title: ToolLibraries Object
description: The ToolLibraries object provides utilities to access, import and update tool libraries.

methods.name: childAssetURLs
methods.description: Get all assets under given URL.

methods.name: childFolderURLs
methods.description: Get all library folders under given URL.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createFolder
methods.description: Create a new folder in the library. Create the folder under given parent URL with given folder name. Add counting suffix, in case a folder with given name already exists. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only. Also throws an error if given folder name is empty.

methods.name: createQuery
methods.description: Creates a new ToolQuery that is used to query the library for tools matching the query.

methods.name: deleteAsset
methods.description: Delete asset by URL from the library. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid asset or the URL is read-only.

methods.name: deleteFolder
methods.description: Delete folder by URL from the library. Any content of the folder will also be deleted. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only.

methods.name: displayName
methods.description: Get the localized display name for a given URL. The URL must point to a folder.

methods.name: doesPathExist
methods.description: Checks if the given URL points to an existing folder or asset in the library.

methods.name: importToolLibrary
methods.description: Import a given ToolLibrary from a specific location. The imported ToolLibrary can be accessed through this ToolLibraries object. Throws an error, if the given URL is read-only.

methods.name: toolLibraryAtURL
methods.description: Get a specific ToolLibrary by given URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.

methods.name: updateToolLibrary
methods.description: Update ToolLibrary in ToolLibraries. Overrides the URL by given ToolLibrary. Throws an error if the URL does not already point to an existing ToolLibrary.

methods.name: urlByLocation
methods.description: Get the URL for a given LibraryLocations.

properties.name: assetTypeName
properties.description: Get the name of the asset type which can be accessed by the library.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Basic Milling Workflow Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the creation of a basic milling workflow from scriptDemonstrates creating a setup, searching tool library to retrieve a tool, create a couple of machining operations and a NC program, ready for post processing.Use the 2D Strategies model from the Fusion CAM Samples folder as your CAD model.

related_samples.name: Hole and Pocket Recognition API Sample
related_samples.description: This sample script demonstrates three different methods for feature recognition: one for holes and two for pockets.The script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to demonstrate the three methods. After the features are recognised they are coloured and milling and drilling operations are created for each feature.RecognizedHoleGroup returns a list of BRepFaces that can be used as selections for the drilling operation. RecognizedPocket also return some BRepFaces but it needs additional processing before the output can be used for creating machining operations.The sample script demonstrates a couple of different methods, including finding the pocket BRepFaces and creating sketches from the recognized pockets.This script works only if the Manufacturing Extension is active.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Turning Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Turning Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by opening a simple component which is then used to describe a basic turning workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

related_samples.name: Wood Routing Workflow Sample
related_samples.description: This script demonstrates routing wood panels.When running the sample, it assumes you have an open design containing one or more "panels" oriented flat in the X-Y plane. The script creates a setup and a 2D contour operation with tabs to route the panels from a standard sheet.

assetTypeName.title: ToolLibraries.assetTypeName Property
assetTypeName.description: Get the name of the asset type which can be accessed by the library.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = toolLibraries_var.assetTypeName

isValid.title: ToolLibraries.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = toolLibraries_var.isValid

objectType.title: ToolLibraries.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = toolLibraries_var.objectType

childAssetURLs.title: ToolLibraries.childAssetURLs Method
childAssetURLs.description: Get all assets under given URL.
childAssetURLs.return_description: Returns list of asset URLs at given location.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the parent folder.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

childFolderURLs.title: ToolLibraries.childFolderURLs Method
childFolderURLs.description: Get all library folders under given URL.
childFolderURLs.return_description: Returns list of folder URLs at given location.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the parent folder.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.

classType.title: ToolLibraries.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createFolder.title: ToolLibraries.createFolder Method
createFolder.description: Create a new folder in the library. Create the folder under given parent URL with given folder name. Add counting suffix, in case a folder with given name already exists. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only. Also throws an error if given folder name is empty.
createFolder.return_description: Returns the URL to the newly created folder

parameters.name: parentUrl
parameters.description: The parent URL for the folder to be created.

parameters.name: folderName
parameters.description: Name of the new folder to be created. Must not be empty.

createQuery.title: ToolLibraries.createQuery Method
createQuery.description: Creates a new ToolQuery that is used to query the library for tools matching the query.
createQuery.return_description: Returns a new ToolQuery. The query is predefined by given parameter.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The location specifies the LibraryLocations where to search.

deleteAsset.title: ToolLibraries.deleteAsset Method
deleteAsset.description: Delete asset by URL from the library. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid asset or the URL is read-only.
deleteAsset.return_description: Returns true if asset was deleted successfully, false otherwise

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the asset that should be removed.

deleteFolder.title: ToolLibraries.deleteFolder Method
deleteFolder.description: Delete folder by URL from the library. Any content of the folder will also be deleted. Throw an error if given URL does not point to a valid folder or the URL is read-only.
deleteFolder.return_description: Returns true if folder was deleted successfully, false otherwise

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the folder that should be removed.

displayName.title: ToolLibraries.displayName Method
displayName.description: Get the localized display name for a given URL. The URL must point to a folder.
displayName.return_description: Returns localized display name for the folder. Returns empty string for invalid URL.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL that defines the path to a folder.

doesPathExist.title: ToolLibraries.doesPathExist Method
doesPathExist.description: Checks if the given URL points to an existing folder or asset in the library.
doesPathExist.return_description: Returns true if the URL points to an existing folder or asset, false otherwise.

importToolLibrary.title: ToolLibraries.importToolLibrary Method
importToolLibrary.description: Import a given ToolLibrary from a specific location. The imported ToolLibrary can be accessed through this ToolLibraries object. Throws an error, if the given URL is read-only.
importToolLibrary.return_description: Returns the URL of the newly imported tool library, or null if the import failed.

parameters.name: toolLibrary
parameters.description: The ToolLibrary that should be imported.

parameters.name: destinationUrl
parameters.description: The URL to the parent folder where the imported tool library will be saved.

parameters.name: libraryName
parameters.description: The name of the library that should be created due to the import. If the specified name already exists, a number will be added to the name to ensure it is unique.

toolLibraryAtURL.title: ToolLibraries.toolLibraryAtURL Method
toolLibraryAtURL.description: Get a specific ToolLibrary by given URL. Returns null, if the URL does not exist.
toolLibraryAtURL.return_description: Returns the ToolLibrary for a valid URL, returns null otherwise.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the ToolLibrary to be loaded.

updateToolLibrary.title: ToolLibraries.updateToolLibrary Method
updateToolLibrary.description: Update ToolLibrary in ToolLibraries. Overrides the URL by given ToolLibrary. Throws an error if the URL does not already point to an existing ToolLibrary.
updateToolLibrary.return_description: Returns true if asset was updated successfully, false otherwise.

parameters.name: url
parameters.description: The URL to the existing asset in the ToolLibraries that should be updated.

parameters.name: toolLibrary
parameters.description: The ToolLibrary that should be persisted.

urlByLocation.title: ToolLibraries.urlByLocation Method
urlByLocation.description: Get the URL for a given LibraryLocations.
urlByLocation.return_description: Returns the URL for given location.

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: The LibraryLocations to be converted into an URL.

related_samples.name: Manufacturing Workflow API Sample
related_samples.description: Manufacturing Workflow API SampleThis sample script starts by creating a simple component which is then used to describe a milling workflow. It creates a setup, a few operations, pick some tools from a Fusion sample tool library using loops and queries and ends up post-processing the operations out using an NC Program.
